summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships cross-platform crypto-clipboard hijacker with persistence
details: This package homoglyphs the popular @juggle/resize-observer (jaggle vs juggle, plus pluralized name 'resizeobserves') and copies its README verbatim, but ships an unrelated payload. The npm postinstall script (npm-install.js) locates pip and runs `pip install --upgrade --break-system-packages <PKG_DIR>` to globally install a bundled Python package (clipboard_guardian), bypassing PEP 668 distro protections. On Linux it additionally invokes `apt-get install -y xclip` (and pacman/dnf fallbacks) without consent. After install, it writes cross-platform persistence under deceptive OS-vendor names — a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` on Linux, a launchd plist `com.apple.python.runtime.plist` on macOS, and a Windows Scheduled Task `PyRuntimeBroker` — and enables/starts them via systemctl/launchctl/PowerShell. The Python payload (guardian.py) polls the clipboard every 0.4s, detects wallet addresses across ~40 chains (BTC, ETH, XMR, SOL, TRX, TON, etc.), and silently rewrites them to hardcoded attacker addresses (e.g. ethereum 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18), redirecting user funds. The process renames itself to `python3-dbus-helper` / `com.apple.python.runtime` / `Python Runtime Broker` and pauses when process-monitor tools (htop, taskmgr, Activity Monitor) are running, to evade observation. Multiple independent supply-chain block fingerprints are present: typosquat with cover-story README, install-time global Python install bypassing distro safeguards, deceptive cross-platform persistence, and a silent-relay clipper that steals cryptocurrency from any installer who copies a wallet address.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
